added: POST /set-fabric-color, /set-fabric-texture, /set-fabric-graphic + FabricDispatcher (PostMessage-based, non-blocking dispatch) — texture pipeline support

Windows: crash-forensic TraceLog — BEGIN/END markers written to disk before/after import-avatar-avt, import-pattern, create-seam, and (new in this version) set-fabric-color/texture/graphic, so a hard SEH crash leaves an unmatched BEGIN identifying exactly which CLO call was in flight

Windows: MIRRA_PLUGIN_LOG_DIR build-time constant, generated by build_plugin.py's generate_build_info_header() from LOGS_DIR (clo_workspace/logs on whichever machine runs the build) — computed fresh per build, per machine
changed: Windows + Mac: REST server port reverted from 50600 back to 50505 — svr.listen() on both platforms, all 3 Python client defaults (avatar_runtime/client.py, adapters/clo_native_client.py, native_vto/client.py), native_vto/pipeline.py's display string, and both DoFunction() message-box strings on Windows, all updated together

Windows: has_avatar_state_readback / has_avatar_avt_export in /capabilities now sourced from the real Phase 3 startup probe (RunCapabilityProbesOnce, g_capabilityAvatarStateReadback / g_capabilityAvatarAvtExport), not hardcoded — same as Mac already did
Windows: TraceLogPath() now resolves via MIRRA_PLUGIN_LOG_DIR instead of a hardcoded machine-specific absolute path (was 'C:/Users/Anant/mirra-mvp/...', broken on every other machine)
Windows: queue drain timer confirmed at 200ms (matching Mac's QTimer interval); 2 stale code comments still describing 500ms corrected to match
fixed: Windows: ImportAvatar(), ImportDXF(), and AddSeamlinePairGroup() were each being invoked TWICE per request — an incomplete merge had kept both sides of a conflicted hunk instead of merging them. Each now calls its CLO SDK function exactly once.

Windows: create-seam handler had a dangling, unclosed PATTERN_API->AddSeamlinePairGroup( call left over from the same incomplete merge (a genuine syntax error — comments and local variables sat inside an open argument list), plus a broken `asyncResult.message = asyncResult.success` with no ternary continuation. Rebuilt as a single, complete call + message assignment.
Windows: the 3 fabric handlers referenced an undefined `result` variable (the rest of ProcessCommandQueue had been renamed to `asyncResult` in a parallel change); renamed to match.

Windows: ProcessCommandQueue had two duplicate `catch (...)` handlers back to back (MSVC C2739 — would not compile); merged into one, keeping the syncResult assignment so sync-read callers still get a real error payload instead of an empty one on a non-std::exception throw.
Windows + Mac: /capabilities response had duplicate has_avatar_state_readback / has_avatar_avt_export / notes keys from an incomplete merge resolution; deduplicated to one of each.
known_issues: set-fabric-graphic accepts u/v/scale (per plugin_contract.json's schema) but ProcessCommandQueue never applies them — it calls the same dispatchTexture() used for plain textures, so a graphic always behaves like a full texture replacement, never a positioned/scaled overlay. See .agent/clo-avatar-vto/known-issue-fabric-graphic-placement.md. Not introduced by this version — pre-existing since the fabric feature was first added.
Mac has no TraceLog equivalent yet. Deliberately deferred — do not port until this Windows build is rebuilt and confirmed working (see .agent/clo-avatar-vto/merge-followup-clo-workspace.md).
Native avatar measurement CSV import remains under investigation (carried forward from earlier versions — unchanged in this one).

v_1.2.0.json's own changelog was stale relative to its actual source (missing the fabric feature and Phase 3 SEH hardening entirely) — partly what motivated writing this version file accurately rather than continuing to drift.

notes: This version has been merged and source-reviewed but NOT YET BUILT OR TESTED. Windows only for this cycle — build via `python clo_workspace/build_plugin.py`, then verify /health, /capabilities, and the trace log before running Step 1 and Step 3 pipelines. Mac source was updated for port/message-string consistency only (not rebuilt, not TraceLog-ported) — marked 'pending', not 'in_sync', until it gets its own build + verification pass.
